disclaimer: Collected manually from Nykaa Fashion website and app. Nykaa Fashion targets urban/metro premium customers (₹800-2,500 price band). D2C brands dominate. Trends here validate demand at higher price points — trickle-down potential to value-fashion.
platform_note: Nykaa Fashion is an originator of many ethnic wear trends through D2C brands (Suta, The Indian Ethnic Co., Aaboli, etc.). If a trend succeeds here at ₹1,200+, a well-executed version at ₹599-799 can capture mass-market demand. Editorial content (look-books, occasion guides) provides intent data before transaction data.

trend_notes.chanderi-straight: Strongest signal on Nykaa. 3 premium brands backing Chanderi at ₹1,200-2,200 with near-zero discount. This validates trickle-down potential — if premium consumers pay full price, value-fashion version at ₹699 has proven demand pyramid.
trend_notes.ikat-anarkali: Karagiri's ikat at ₹1,650 with 10% off is a clean signal. Nykaa editorial positioning in 'Handloom Heroes' suggests they see ikat as a rising heritage trend, not a discount play.
trend_notes.organza-embroidered: Strong premium demand at ₹1,890. However: narrow occasion window. Nykaa positions as wedding/festive only. Value-fashion version at ₹799 should be pre-festive, not year-round.
trend_notes.ajrakh-cotton: Aaboli at ₹1,250 with just 5% discount is the cleanest demand signal. Low discount + limited stock = genuine demand exceeding supply. But volume is tiny (65 reviews). Early adopter niche.
trend_notes.blockprint-cotton: Notably absent from Nykaa Fashion. Block print is too basic/commoditized for Nykaa's premium positioning. This is NOT a negative — it means the value-fashion market owns this category without premium competition.
